subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Analysis of Functions
stimulus: Let $f$ be the function defined by $f(x) = kx^2 + \ln x$ for $x > 0$, where $k$ is a constant.

parts.prompt: For what value of the constant $k$ does $f$ have a critical point at $x = 1$? For this value of $k$, determine whether $f$ has a relative minimum, relative maximum, or neither at $x = 1$. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: For what value of the constant $k$ does $f$ have a critical point at $x = 1$? For this value of $k$, determine whether $f$ has a relative minimum, relative maximum, or neither at $x = 1$. Justify your answer.

parts.prompt: For a certain value of the constant $k$, the graph of $f$ has a point of inflection on the $x$-axis. Find this value of $k$.
rubric_criteria: For a certain value of the constant $k$, the graph of $f$ has a point of inflection on the $x$-axis. Find this value of $k$.
